meta.reportLabel: Mon Report — RBNZ binary BACK ON: ~75% hike priced for Wed (22/28 economists say 2.50%; ANZ/BNZ hike, ASB/Westpac hold) - the weekend '<30%' read flipped in a session; JPY follow-through FADED (back to ~161.8, no Monday ambush - trade reverts to intervention lottery); WTI fresh $68.3 low; US-Iran talks resume Jul 11.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: The RBNZ binary is back on - violently: ~75% of a Wednesday hike is now priced (22/28 economists say 2.50%; ANZ tips three hikes starting July) after the weekend's 'hold near-certain' read died in a session - Wednesday is again the week's coin-flip, with the HOLD now the pain trade. The JPY follow-through faded (no Monday ambush, spot back ~161.8) so JPY shorts revert from momentum to ambush-lottery deep in the proven MoF zone. USD holds its hawkish-hold floor (zero cuts, Sept ~50%) into today's ISM and the Jul 14 CPI; the oil glut (WTI ~$68.3 fresh low) keeps CAD weakest; US-Iran talks resume Jul 11.
meta.nextBigEvent: Wed Jul 8: RBNZ - binary BACK ON (~75% hike priced, 22/28 economists; ASB/Westpac still say hold) + Fed June minutes

dailyRead: The RBNZ binary is back on - violently. What printed as 'hold near-certain' over the weekend has flipped in a session: 22 of 28 polled economists now expect a hike to 2.50% Wednesday, with market odds ~75% (ANZ - now tipping three hikes starting July - and BNZ call the hike; ASB and Westpac still say hold; the NZIER shadow board is split line-ball). Wednesday is once again the week's genuine coin-flip, and ANZ's own base case is a 'neutral-to-dovish' hike designed to minimise the reaction. Meanwhile the JPY follow-through FADED: USD/JPY drifted back to ~161.8 through the Monday Asia open with no MoF ambush on the thin tape - the momentum signal failed even as spot walks deeper into the proven intervention zone. USD holds its hawkish-hold floor (zero cuts priced, Sept ~50%, June CPI Jul 14 the arbiter), CAD stays weakest (WTI ~$68.3 fresh low, glut intact), and Doha diplomacy now has a date: talks resume July 11, after the funeral ends July 9. Cleanest board: USD/CAD and AUD/CAD longs; NZD trades are event trades until Wednesday 02:00 UTC.

strength.tag: Sept ~50%; ISM today, CPI Jul 14 decide
strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May); June NFP +57k big miss, unemp 4.2%; Fed 3.50-3.75% hawkish hold, zero 2026 cuts priced; Sept hike odds ~50% drifting toward October; Warsh's 'unanimous and unambiguous' price-stability framing floors it.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hawkish hold into the Jul 28-29 FOMC; Sept ~50% and drifting post-NFP but the zero-cut floor holds. Today's ISM Services is the first post-NFP pulse; the Jun CPI (Jul 14) re-arms or kills the hike tail. Risk: a second soft print in a row turns the crack into a trend. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May, up from 2.5%); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes); quiet tape, thesis unchanged.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish - the services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR round. Risk: a soft activity/labor wave re-splits the MPC. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.drivers: CPI 3.1% Q1 (RBNZ sees >4% Q2); unemp 5.3% Q1. RBNZ 2.25%; May was a 3-3 split decided by Governor Breman. RE-REPRICED: ~75% hike odds for Jul 8, 22/28 economists expect 2.50%; ANZ (three hikes from July) and BNZ hike, ASB/Westpac hold, NZIER shadow board line-ball.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hike is the base case Wednesday but the vote splits the street; ANZ's script is a 'neutral-to-dovish' hike - raise, then sound noncommittal. The weekend '<30%' read is dead. Risk: a HOLD flushes the 75% premium hard; even a delivered hike can sell off on dovish guidance. Next RBNZ Jul 8.

strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6% (up from 3.4%); headline 4.0%; unemp 4.5% loosening. RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes). FORWARD SHIFT: the Aug 11 hike is priced only ~36% and increasingly called an outlier; Dec ~67%.
strength.forward: ⚖️ The sticky-core floor holds but the near-term hawkish kicker faded - RBA seen on hold through Q3. Risk: a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arms August; risk-off flushes the commodity beta. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. The momentum signal failed: no MoF ambush on Monday's thin tape and USD/JPY drifted back to ~161.8, giving back Friday's gains.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Spot is now deeper in the proven MoF zone (April's Y11.7tn round) and Katayama's 'any time' stands - the trade reverts from momentum follow-through to ambush-lottery; the BoJ path (Jul 31) stays hawkish. Risk: for JPY shorts a no-warning strike; for JPY longs the ~250bp+ carry bleed with no catalyst date. Next BoJ Jul 31.

strength.tag: flash 2.8% cooling BUT core up to 2.5%
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11, first since 2023), meeting-by-meeting.
strength.forward: ⚖️ The cooling headline guts Jul 23 urgency (hold expected) but rising core keeps a Sept hike alive - the dovish EUR read is not clean. Risk: a hawkish Jul 23 tone on the core print squeezes EUR shorts. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.tag: WTI fresh $68.3 low; Jul 10 jobs next
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); unemp 6.6%; May +88k (best since Dec-24) but jobs flat YTD, wages 3.0% cooling; technical recession. BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold. WTI ~$68.3 fresh Monday low; Saudi exports at 90% of pre-war baseline, Iranian crude stacking at sea.
strength.forward: 🔻 Glut intact and deepening at the margin - structurally offered; BoC sidelined Jul 15; Friday's June jobs print (whether May's +88k was noise) is the near catalyst. Risk: a Hormuz incident re-spikes oil; a second strong jobs print firms the floor. Next BoC Jul 15.

geopolitics: Funeral week, midpoint - and the diplomacy now has dates. Khamenei's rites run July 4-9 (Tehran -> Qom -> burial at the Imam Reza Shrine in Mashhad; the largest state funeral in Iran's history), with the Doha talks paused and now scheduled to RESUME JULY 11. The last round produced claims of a partial release of frozen Iranian funds - asserted by Tehran, denied by US officials - itself a read on how contested the deal's plumbing remains. The standing collision point is unchanged: the June 17 MoU obliges Iran to 'best efforts' toll-free Hormuz transit for 60 days only (lapsing ~mid-August), and Iranian officials keep repeating that fees will 'definitely' come - friendly nations get 'special treatment' - while Washington rejects the concept outright. Oil is not pricing this: WTI printed a fresh ~$68.3 low Monday, Brent ~$71-72, Saudi exports hold at 90% of the pre-war baseline, UAE restored, Iranian crude stacking at sea, record Russian exports. Transmission: falling oil -> eroding energy-CPI floors -> less CB hawkishness at the margin - though Wellington just showed the limit of that logic, with the RBNZ's hawks (and 22/28 economists) looking through the oil slide at domestic inflation. CAD still holds the bag; falling oil remains the GER40 tailwind (record highs) and does NOT buy a Fed cut (US core 2.9%; the disinflation is energy-led). Through Thursday the tail is the funeral vacuum itself - an incident with negotiators sidelined would re-spike oil into a market that has crushed the war premium (rescuing CAD, hurting AUD/NZD/GER40, handing USD and JPY competing haven bids); after Saturday the tail migrates to the talks themselves and the mid-August toll clock.
